frames.narrationSummary: Initialize an empty deque that will keep candidate indices in decreasing value order.
Initialize the output list for completed window maximums.
Advance to index 0 for the next window transition.
Move the focus to nums[0] = 1.
The deque front still belongs to the current window, so keep it.
Push index 0 so it can compete in later windows.
The window is not full yet, so do not emit an output.
Advance to index 1 for the next window transition.
Move the focus to nums[1] = 3.
The deque front still belongs to the current window, so keep it.
Deque back index 0 is dominated by 3, so pop it.
Pop dominated index 0 from the deque back.
Push index 1 so it can compete in later windows.
The window is not full yet, so do not emit an output.
Advance to index 2 for the next window transition.
Move the focus to nums[2] = -1.
The deque front still belongs to the current window, so keep it.
The deque back is larger than the current value, so it can stay as a candidate.
Push index 2 so it can compete in later windows.
The first full window is ready, so emit its maximum.
Append 3 from deque front index 1 to the result.
Advance to index 3 for the next window transition.
Move the focus to nums[3] = -3.
The deque front still belongs to the current window, so keep it.
The deque back is larger than the current value, so it can stay as a candidate.
Push index 3 so it can compete in later windows.
The first full window is ready, so emit its maximum.
Append 3 from deque front index 1 to the result.
Advance to index 4 for the next window transition.
Move the focus to nums[4] = 5.
Deque front index 1 is stale, so it must leave the window.
Remove stale index 1 from the deque front.
The deque front still belongs to the current window, so keep it.
Deque back index 3 is dominated by 5, so pop it.
Pop dominated index 3 from the deque back.
Deque back index 2 is dominated by 5, so pop it.
Pop dominated index 2 from the deque back.
Push index 4 so it can compete in later windows.
The first full window is ready, so emit its maximum.
Append 5 from deque front index 4 to the result.
Every index has been processed, so the output list is complete.
Return the completed maxima list [3, 3, 5].
